author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXXIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a short Psalm, but one well known and quoted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, how good and how pleasant is it, that brethren should dwell together in unity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So sweet is that sound, that even they who know not the Psalter, sing that verse. …

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these same words of the Psalter, this sweet sound, that honeyed melody, as well of the mind as of the hymn, did even beget the Monasteries.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By this sound were stirred up the brethren who longed to dwell together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This verse was their trumpet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It sounded through the whole earth, and they who had been divided, were gathered together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The summons of God, the summons of the Holy Spirit, the summons of the Prophets, were not heard in Judah, yet were heard through the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were deaf to that sound, amid whom it was sung; they were found with their ears open, of whom it was said, “They shall see him, who were not told of him; they shall understand who heard not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet, most beloved, if we reflect, the very blessing hath sprung from that wall of circumcision.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For have all the Jews perished? and whence were the Apostles, the sons of the Prophets, the sons of the exiles?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He speaks as to them who know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence those five hundred, who saw the Lord after His resurrection, whom the Apostle Paul commemorates?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence those hundred and twenty, who were together in one place after the resurrection of the Lord, and His ascension into heaven, on whom when gathered into one place the Holy Spirit descended on the day of Pentecost, sent down from heaven, sent, even as He was promised?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All were from thence, and they first dwelt together in unity; who sold all they had, and laid the price of their goods at the Apostles’ feet, as is read in the Acts of the Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And distribution was made to each one as he had need, and none called anything his own, but they had all things common.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is “together in unity”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They had, he says, one mind and one heart God-wards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So they were the first who heard, Behold how good and how pleasant is it, that brethren dwell together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were the first to hear, but heard it not alone.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the words of this Psalm was taken the name of Monks, that no one may reproach you who are Catholics by reason of the name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When you with justice reproach heretics by reason of the Circelliones, that they may be saved by shame, they reproach you on the score of the Monks.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moreover, beloved, there are they who are false Monks, and we know men of this kind; but the pious brotherhood is not annulled, because of them who profess to be what they are not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are false Monks, as there are false men among the Clergy, and among the faithful.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since the Psalm says, “Behold, how good and how pleasant is it, that brethren should dwell together in one,” why then should we not call Monks so? for Monos is one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not one in any manner, for a man in a crowd is one, but though he can be called one along with others, he cannot be Monos, that is, alone, for Monos means “one alone.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then who thus live together as to make one man, so that they really possess what is written, “one mind and one heart,” many bodies, but not many minds; many bodies, but not many hearts; can rightly be called Monos, that is, one alone.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Psalm tell us what they are like.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As the ointment on the head, which descended to the beard, to Aaron’s beard, which descended to the fringe of his garment” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was Aaron?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is a priest, except that one Priest, who entered into the Holy of Holies?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is that priest, save Him, who was at once Victim and Priest? save Him who when he found nothing clean in the world to offer, offered Himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The ointment is on his head, because Christ is one whole with the Church, but the ointment comes from the head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Head is Christ crucified and buried; He rose again, and ascended into heaven; and the Holy Spirit came from the head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the beard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The beard signifies the courageous; the beard distinguishes the grown men, the earnest, the active, the vigorous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that when we describe such, we say, he is a bearded man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus that ointment descended first upon the Apostles, descended upon those who bore the first assaults of the world, and therefore the Holy Spirit descended on them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they who first began to dwell together in unity, suffered persecution, but because the ointment descended to the beard, they suffered, but were not conquered.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As the dew of Hermon, which fell upon the hills of Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He would have it understood, my brethren, that it is of God’s grace that brethren dwell together in unity.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But ye should know what Hermon is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a mountain far distant from Jerusalem, that is, from Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And so it is strange that he says thus: As the dew of Hermon, which fell upon the mountains of Sion, since mount Hermon is far distant from Jerusalem, for it is said to be over Jordan.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us then seek out some interpretation of Hermon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The word is Hebrew, and we learn its meaning from them who know that language.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hermon is said to mean, a light set on a high place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from Christ comes the dew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No light is set on a high place, save Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is He set on high?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First on the cross, afterwards in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Set on high on the cross when He was humbled; humbled, but His humiliation could not but be high.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The ministry of man grew less and less, as was signified in John; the ministry of God in our Lord Jesus Christ increased, as was shown at their birth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former was born, as the tradition of the Church shows, on the 24th of June, when the days begin to shorten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord was born on the 25th of December, when the days begin to lengthen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here John himself confessing, “He must increase, but I must decrease.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the passion of each shows this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord was exalted on the cross; John was diminished by beheading.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus the light set on high is Christ, whence is the dew of Hermon.…But if he have the dew of Hermon, which fell on the hill of Sion, he is quiet, peaceable, humble, submissive, pouring forth prayer in place of murmuring.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For murmurers are admirably described in a certain passage of the Scriptures, “The heart of a fool is as the wheel of a cart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the meaning of “the heart of a fool is as the wheel of a cart”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It carries hay, and creaks.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The wheel of a cart cannot cease from creaking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus there are many brethren, who do not dwell together, save in the body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who are they who dwell together?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They of whom it is said, “And they had one mind and one heart towards God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because there the Lord commanded blessing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where did He command it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Among the brethren who dwell together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There He enjoined blessing, there they who dwell with one heart bless God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou blessest not God in division of heart.…Art thou straitened on earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Depart, have thy habitation in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall I, a man clothed in flesh, enslaved to the flesh, thou wilt say, have my habitation in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First go in heart, whither thou wouldest follow in the body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not hear, “Lift up your hearts,” with a deaf ear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Keep thy heart lifted up, and no one will straiten thee in heaven.
